mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive month-over-month trend in industrial production signals expanding real output capacity, which supports corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value=104.1152 as of 2014-08-01 showing continued expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month trend and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO trend periods show no statistically significant excess return versus baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points within a single month after a prolonged near-zero period, markets price in an accelerated tightening cycle, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases > 0.25pp in a single month following a period below 0.25% for at least 12 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS held at 0.09 as of 2014-08-01, near zero-lower-bound with anticipated 2015 liftoff
regime_hint: monetary policy liftoff from zero lower bound
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered liftoff instances, or Rank IC is below 0.02 across walk-forward folds spanning the 2015-2016 tightening episode.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth (PAYEMS accelerating) and declining unemployment (UNRATE falling) while FEDFUNDS remains near zero signals an overheating labor market that raises rate-hike expectations, jointly producing a negative equity reaction over the next month that would not occur from either series alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change > 200k AND UNRATE falls > 0.2pp over 3 months AND FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.1% and FEDFUNDS at 0.09 as of Aug 2014 with steady payroll gains
regime_hint: late-cycle labor tightening prior to first rate hike
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce non-negative mean 20-day forward returns, or the interaction term's marginal predictive power (measured via nested model Rank IC improvement) is below 0.01 versus single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing market recovery regime, sustained increases in housing starts (HOUST) reflect strengthening consumer confidence and construction-linked economic activity, which propagate through the economy over a quarter and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised_value=1085.0 thousand units as of July 2014 indicating post-recession recovery levels
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST 6-month trend and 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 during the 2012-2016 housing recovery window, or HOUST uptrend periods underperform baseline SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects robust near-term consumer spending, which is rapidly incorporated into short-horizon equity pricing and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage_value=444377.0 million as of Aug 2014 showing continued consumer spending growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly RSAFS growth and 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS surprises fail to generate statistically significant excess short-term returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below a structural threshold near 6.0%, indicating approach toward full employment, wage-pressure and inflation-hedging expectations begin repricing equities negatively over the following quarter due to anticipated policy tightening.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.0% for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.1% as of Aug 2014, approaching the 6.0% threshold
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following UNRATE sub-6.0% crossings are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.02 across post-crossing walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI inflation combined with a stationary near-zero FEDFUNDS rate compresses real interest rates, creating a negative real-yield environment that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; this effect requires the joint condition since inflation alone without rate stasis would instead signal tightening risk.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised_value=237.498 and FEDFUNDS=0.09 as of mid-2014
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods show negative mean 20-day forward SPX returns, or the interaction model's Rank IC improvement over single-series CPIAUCSL model is below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: During a mid-cycle economic expansion regime, upward revisions to real GDP growth (GDPC1) confirm sustained aggregate demand strength, reinforcing investor risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; this relationship weakens or reverses in late-cycle or contraction regimes where growth surprises raise tightening fears.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 large upward revision from 15994.3 to 18185.911 reflecting methodology change and continued expansion as of mid-2014
failure_condition: During mid-cycle expansion periods, positive GDPC1 growth surprises fail to produce statistically significant positive 60-day forward returns (p > 0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows within the expansion regime.
